todaysEvents.summary: Brent posts worst month since 2020: -19% in May on deal hopes; Trump ends Situation Room meeting without announcing final determination (May 29)
todaysEvents.delta: Down: Brent closed -1.77% to $92.05 (CNBC confirmed); WTI -1.73% to $87.36. Brent posted -19% in May - worst monthly loss since March 2020. Trump ended WH Situation Room meeting without announcing 'final determination' on Iran deal. World Bank: Brent avg $86 in 2026, $70 in 2027. Oil market pricing in aggressive deal probability despite lingering uncertainty.

todaysEvents.summary: Trump lays out tough Iran demands on Truth Social; Fars disputes deal text; $12B frozen assets demand (May 29)
todaysEvents.delta: Neutral: Trump demanded on Truth Social: Iran must reject nukes, open Hormuz immediately with no tolls, clear mines in 30 days, allow US to destroy enriched uranium. Fars pushed back: no toll-free clause in text, no nuclear dismantlement clause. Fars insists $12B frozen assets release is 'most important part' of agreement. White House did not respond. Deal text disputes underscore fragility of MOU.

todaysEvents.summary: Iran fires ballistic missiles at Kuwait, attack drones at Strait; UBS sees 'little evidence' of flow improvement (May 28-29)
todaysEvents.delta: Neutral: Despite deal optimism, Iran continued military operations: ballistic missiles at Kuwait, attack drones toward Strait (May 28-29 per CNBC/UBS). UBS: Iran crude loadings May <0.3M bpd (down from 1.5M April). Little evidence of any short-term improvement in vessel traffic. Bob Parker (ICMA): oil $90-100 for 'at least next couple months'; Hormuz opening 'only partial' due to infrastructure damage.

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Trump ended Situation Room meeting without announcing final determination (CNBC May 29). 60-day MOU framework exists (Axios, CNBC) but Trump demands tough conditions: nuclear prohibition, no-toll Hormuz, mine removal, enriched uranium destruction. Fars disputes text: no toll-free or nuclear clause, insists on $12B frozen assets. If Trump signs MOU: Brent retreats toward $80-85/bbl; P&I restoration enables commercial traffic; Ras Laffan restart ~late Aug 2026. Increased from D87 25% — MOU framework + oil market pricing aggressive deal probability suggests higher base case.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Deal signed but implementation crawls: Trump demands vs Fars rebuttals show wide gap on nuclear dismantlement, frozen assets ($12B), and Hormuz tolls. Phase 1 Hormuz partial reopening while nuclear sequencing dispute delays Phase 2; mine-clearing ~6 months delays full commercial recovery; P&I clubs restore conditionally; oil stabilises $85-105/bbl. TTF €42-50/MWh; JKM $15-20/MMBtu; iTraxx Asia IG 165-200bp. Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026 even on deal. Dominant scenario (45%) — partial normalisation, not resolution.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Deal collapses: Trump demands incompatible with Iran's $12B frozen assets demand + Fars text dispute. MOU framework fragile — any collapse before signature triggers military escalation and re-pricing. Brent spikes $125-165/bbl; Kharg Island seizure or GCC production targeting; Malaysia July-August supply crisis; iTraxx spikes to 230-260bp. Tail (25%) — reduced from D87 28% on MOU framework progress and oil market pricing indicating higher deal probability; but $12B frozen assets impasse + nuclear dismantlement dispute are collapse triggers.
